title: alyssum atlanticum
intro: Alyssum atlanticum, also known as Creeping Alyssum, is a low-growing, mat-forming perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its tiny white flowers and delicate foliage. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Alyssum atlanticum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Alyssum atlanticum can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
